---
artifact_id: ESE111-CH04-PROJ
pair_id: ESE111-CH04-PROJ
lang: en
pair_revision: 0.1.0
semantic_id: ESE111-CH04-PROJ
id: ESE111-CH04-PROJ-en
resource_type: project-brief
language: en
paired_with: ESE111-CH04-PROJ-fr
version: 0.1.0
lifecycle: drafting
outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CH04-LO03, ESE111-CH04-LO04, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
estimated_student_time_min: 90
safety_level: L1
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
technical_contract:
  outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CH04-LO03, ESE111-CH04-LO04, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
  equations: [KCL signed current sum equals zero, KVL signed loop voltage sum equals zero, v equals R times i, power equals v times i]
  values: {source_v: 6.0, r1_ohm: 10000, r2_ohm: 22000, r3_ohm: 33000, r4_baseline_ohm: 15000, r4_redesign_ohm: 22000}
  safety_limits: {source_max_v: 6.1, current_limit_max_a: 0.02, dmm_current_input_permitted: false}
  expected_ranges: {baseline_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.087, 1.155], redesign_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.412, 1.500], source_current_simulated_a: [0.000229, 0.000244]}
  rubric_total: 28
  answer_key_digest: pending-restricted-store
  figure_ids: [ESE111-CH04-FIG-001]
---

# Design clinic — Make the repair handoff reproducible

## Need, goal, and boundaries

A second technician must be able to inspect an unfamiliar low-voltage network,
reproduce the diagnosis, and verify a threshold change without relying on oral
memory. Produce a compact repair handoff for the generic fixture. No participant,
partner, field, lamp-performance, product-reliability, or geographic-origin claim
is permitted. No mains, battery modification, radio, hot work, or data collection.

## Requirements and acceptance

- RQ1: named nodes, references, branch values, and current arrows are unambiguous.
- RQ2: a technician can reproduce baseline $V_A,V_B$ and uncertainty decisions.
- RQ3: the diagnostic tree separates at least an open and a wrong-value branch.
- RQ4: the change meets TP-B=1.40–1.52 V and source current ≤0.25 mA.
- RQ5: setup, stop, reset, component recovery, and unresolved limitations are explicit.

Pass only after a peer unfamiliar with your document executes a paper walkthrough
without private coaching and identifies every required checkpoint. Physical
replication remains a later gate; a walkthrough must not be mislabeled as one.

## Architecture, parts, and make/buy statement

Use the controlled source, one DMM capability, breadboard, 1% resistors, and the
shared schematic. Select substitute *capabilities*, not a vendor. Record what the
team authored, what came from program assets, and what remains imported/unknown.
The permitted provenance statement is “student-prepared repair handoff based on
the EMM ESE111 fixture”; it is not “locally made product.”

## Work and individual evidence

Pairs rotate evidence lead and skeptical verifier. Each person submits one original
prediction, one checked equation, one decision-tree branch, and one limitation.
Conduct a requirements review, model review, test-readiness review, peer walkthrough,
and release review. Track changes after each review rather than overwriting history.

Deliver editable schematic reference, one-page test-point map, parameter table,
measurement record, uncertainty statement, diagnostic tree, design trade study,
acceptance matrix, component recovery note, bilingual abstract, and change log.
Use the common rubric. Extensions may add a meter-loading calculator or compare
repair time across two document layouts, but cannot substitute prepared data for
the required later physical evidence.
